Google Announces Gemini 3.6 Flash, Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite and Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber

July 21, 2026

Google introduced Gemini 3.6 Flash, Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber to help developers and customers build AI agents in production with higher token efficiency, lower latency, and reliable performance.

3.6 Flash: A workhorse model that delivers better coding, knowledge work, and multimodal performance while reducing token usage by up to 17% [according to Artificial Analysis Index] — at a lower cost per token than 3.5 Flash.

3.5 Flash-Lite: Google's fastest [according to Artificial Analysis Index], most cost-effective 3.5-class model, significantly outperforming prior Flash-Lite generations for high-throughput execution. [...] July 21, 2026

Google introduced Gemini 3.6 Flash, Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber to help developers and customers build AI agents in production with higher token efficiency, lower latency, and reliable performance.

July 21, 2026

Harness is extending its platform to cover the full AI Agent Development Lifecycle (DLC), giving enterprises a single set of pipelines and controls to build, test, deploy, and run agents the same way they already ship everything else.

Testlio Releases LeoCore

July 20, 2026 [...] 3.5 Flash Cyber: A specialized model built on top of 3.5 Flash, and fine-tuned for finding and fixing cybersecurity vulnerabilities at a lower price per token than larger models. The model will be exclusively available to governments and trusted partners via CodeMender as part of a limited-access pilot program. Separately, Google is also bringing CodeMender's foundational capabilities directly to customers with generally available Gemini models through the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform.

results.title: Introducing Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber
results.content:
3.5 Flash-Lite is the fastest model in the 3.5 series. As measured by Artificial Analysis, it runs at 350 output tokens/s. Priced at $0.3/1M input tokens and $2.5/1M output tokens and with significantly better quality than 3.1 Flash-Lite, 3.5 Flash-Lite offers a strong price-to-performance ratio for developers and customers running high throughput production traffic.

Video 5

3.5 Flash-Lite executes high volume tasks at a lower latency than 3.5 Flash. [...] 3.6 Flash: Our workhorse model that delivers better coding, knowledge work, and multimodal performance. According to the Artificial Analysis Index, it reduces output token usage by 17% compared to 3.5 Flash, and in some benchmarks like DeepSWE by Datacurve, we observe up to 65%, all at a lower cost per output token.
   3.5 Flash-Lite: Our fastest, most cost-effective 3.5-class model, delivering 350 output tokens per second according to the Artificial Analysis Index, also significantly outperforming prior Flash-Lite generations in agentic workflows. [...] Image 8: Quote from JetBrains

## Built with safety

3.6 Flash is shipping with enhanced Frontier Safety safeguards in the domains of Chemical, Biological, Radiological, and Nuclear (CBRN) and cyber offense misuses. These safeguards make the model substantially more resistant to jailbreaks. At the same time, the model has been trained to minimize refusals for beneficial uses.

For more information, see the 3.6 Flash model card.

## 3.5 Flash-Lite: Built to scale agentic workflows

Beyond Flash, we’re also releasing Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite, designed for both low-latency tasks and tasks where high throughput is critical for developers workflows, like agentic search and document processing.

# Google Releases New Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber Models

 Laurent Giret
 Jul 21, 2026
 2

Google is making three new Gemini AI models available today to make up for the delayed release of its Gemini 3.5 Pro model, which was announced at its I/O conference in May. The headliner is the Gemini 3.6 Flash model, but there’s also the fastest and more cost-effective 3.5 Flash-Lite and the cybersecurity-focused 3.5 Flash Cyber.

According to Google, Gemini 3.6 Flash is more efficient and cheaper to use than 3.5 Flash for agentic tasks. The new model also comes with additional safeguards in the domains of Chemical, Biological, Radiological, and Nuclear (CBRN) and cyber offense misuses. [...] Lastly, Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber is Google’s response to the latest flagship cybersecurity models from rivals like OpenAI and Anthropic. Google said that it can find and fix cybersecurity vulnerabilities at “a lower price per token than larger models.”

At the moment, access to Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber is restricted to governments and trusted partners via a limited-access pilot program. Gemini 3.6 Flash and 3.5 Flash-Lite, however, are rolling out to the Gemini app, Google Search,  Google AI Studio, Android Studio, and the Gemini Enterprise app.

results.title: Google Introduces Gemini 3.6 Flash, Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, 3.5 Pro Still Missing - iClarified
results.content:
The workhorse Gemini 3.6 Flash replaces the earlier Gemini 3.5 Flash and delivers better coding, knowledge work, and multimodal reasoning while consuming 17% fewer output tokens, according to the Artificial Analysis Index. Google says the improvements lower both output token costs and the overall cost of running agentic tasks. On the DeepSWE coding benchmark it scores 49%, up from 37% for 3.5 Flash, and its computer-use capabilities have also improved. The model includes enhanced safeguards against cyber and chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear (CBRN) misuse and is available now through the Gemini API, Google AI Studio, the Gemini app, and enterprise platforms. [...] Designed for high-throughput workloads, Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite is the fastest model in the family, delivering 350 output tokens per second at $0.30 per million input tokens. It significantly outperforms 3.1 Flash-Lite on agentic and coding evaluations and even surpasses the original Gemini 3 Flash on benchmarks including SWE-Bench Pro and OSWorld-Verified.  
  
Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber is a fine-tuned version of 3.5 Flash designed to find and fix software vulnerabilities. It will be available only to governments and trusted partners through a limited-access CodeMender pilot program. [...] Google today introduced Gemini 3.6 Flash, Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber, three new AI models focused on speed, efficiency, and specialized security workloads. Missing from the launch was the long-awaited Gemini 3.5 Pro, leaving the flagship model's release timeline uncertain.
